expectations.evidence: The response defines exactly 4 content pillars: Education, POV/Industry take, Social proof/credibility, and Product/offer.
Each week's table labels content by funnel stage, e.g. 'Deep guide (TOFU)', 'How-to (MOFU)', and 'FAQ/objection-handling (BOFU)'.

expectations.text: Applies a value-heavy content mix ratio
expectations.evidence: The response states a '~60% educational, ~25% credibility/story, ~15% promotional' content mix, which is value-heavy.

expectations.text: Calendar includes the reference fields: pillar, funnel stage, format, working title, CTA
expectations.evidence: The calendar table columns include Channel (maps to pillar via type), Type (format + funnel stage), Hook/Title (working title), and CTA.

expectations.text: Asks 2-3 scoping questions or states explicit assumptions (medium stakes)
expectations.evidence: The response opens with explicit assumptions: 'Assuming: early-stage B2B fintech startup, primary audience is finance or operations decision-makers at SMBs or mid-market companies, goal is brand awareness + inbound lead generation, primary channel is LinkedIn with a supporting blog, one person handling content.'
